questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Match the following assuming the rest of the function are constant apart from given variables for ideal gas
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& P V=n R T \\
& \ln P V=\ln n R+\ln T \\
& \ln P=\ln n+\ln \frac{R T}{V} \\
& P V=n R T
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Select the incorrect option about collision frequency(z) for a given gas
options: At constant temperature Z increases with increase in pressure
At constant pressure $\mathbf{Z}$ decreases with increase in temperature
At constant volume Z increases with increase in pressure
At constant volume $\mathbf{Z}$ decreases with increase in temperature
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& Z=\frac{\pi d^2 \sqrt{2} \overline{\mathbf{C}} P}{k T} \\
& Z \propto \overline{\mathbf{C}} \times \frac{\mathbf{P}}{\mathbf{T}} \propto \sqrt{\mathrm{T}} \times \frac{\mathbf{P}}{\mathbf{T}} \propto \frac{\mathbf{P}}{\sqrt{T}}
\end{aligned}$

So, when $T$ is constant $Z \propto P$
When $P$ is constant $Z \propto \frac{1}{\sqrt{T}}$
At constant $\mathrm{V}, \mathrm{Z}$ is governed by $\overline{\mathrm{C}}$, here $\mathbf{Z} \propto \sqrt{\mathbf{P}}$ or $\sqrt{\mathbf{T}}$

So, when volume is constant
$\mathbf{Z} \propto \sqrt{\mathbf{T}} \notin \mathbf{Z} \propto \sqrt{\mathbf{P}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Consider the apparatus shown:
When 'rubber bulb' of medicine dropper will be squeezed:
options: the litmus solution in beaker will turn red with a slight increase in volume
the litmus solution in the beaker will turn red with slight decrease in volume
the litmus solution will rush up through the long tube to create a blue fountain in the Flask
the litmus solution will rush up through the long tube to create a red fountain in the flask
questions.solution: $\mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{g})$ is highly soluble in water and is also acidic in nature.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $1 \mathrm{~m}^3$ container is filled with vapours of 0.40 kg at 1 atm and 400 K . When 0.025 kg of $\mathrm{H}_2$ gas effuses through a pin hole, 0.1 kg of vapours effuse in the same time interval. Select the incorrect statement about the vapours from the given options
options: Vapour is showing negative deviation from ideal gas
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{n H_2}{n_v}=\sqrt{\frac{M_v}{M_{H_2}}} \\
& \frac{0.025 \times M_v}{2 \times 0.1}=\sqrt{\frac{M_v}{2}} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad M_v=32 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \\
& P V=n R T \\
& V=\frac{400 \times 0.0821 \times 400}{32 \times 1} \\
& =410.5 \mathrm{~L}
\end{aligned}$

Which is far less than, the actual volume occupied by gas. So, Z>1
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct about the gas according to "Kinetic molecular theory"?
(i) The mean free path is much higher than the diameter of the gas
(ii) The molecules move in straight lines until they colloide
(iii) The diatomic gas has a relationship between absolute temperature and root mean square speed ( $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{ms}}$ ) as $\mathbf{T}=\frac{\mathbf{M V}_{\text {ms }}^2}{\mathbf{9 R}}$ (M-molar mass, R-universal gas constant)
(iv) Potential energy of the gas has 100\% contribution towards the total energy of the gas
questions.solution: Diatomic gas has three translational and two rotational degree of freedom.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
